title: "local development."
description: "environment setup, dev servers, and local workflow for iris."

# local development.

<div className="docs-hero">
  <div className="docs-hero__row">
    <div>
      <div className="docs-hero__eyebrow">environment</div>
      <h1>run the full stack locally.</h1>
      <p>
        iris runs as two processes locally: a vite frontend on port 3000 and a
        fastapi backend on port 8000. both read from a shared .env at the repo root.
      </p>
    </div>
    <div className="docs-slab">
      <div className="docs-section__eyebrow">prerequisites</div>
      <ul className="docs-mini-list">
        <li>Node.js 20+ or Bun</li>
        <li>Python 3.11+</li>
        <li>FFmpeg + FFprobe</li>
        <li>PostgreSQL (or Docker)</li>
      </ul>
    </div>
  </div>
</div>

## environment variables.

create a `.env` file at the repo root. see `.env.example` for the full list.

key variables:

| variable | purpose | required |
|----------|---------|----------|
| `GEMINI_API_KEY` | gemini vision + text generation | yes (or use stubs) |
| `DATABASE_URL` | postgres connection string | yes |
| `USE_AI_STUBS` | `true` for offline dev, `false` for real AI | no (defaults to true) |
| `VULTR_ACCESS_KEY` / `VULTR_SECRET_KEY` | object storage for media | no (local storage fallback) |
| `SUPABASE_URL` / `SUPABASE_JWT_SECRET` | authentication | no (anon sessions work) |
| `VITE_SUPABASE_URL` / `VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY` | frontend auth client | no |
| `GPU_WORKER_URL` | SAM2 segmentation worker | no |
| `ELEVENLABS_API_KEY` | TTS narration | no |

## starting the frontend.

```bash
cd frontend
bun install
bun run dev
```

runs on `http://localhost:3000`.

## starting the backend.

```bash
cd backend
pip install -r requirements.txt
uvicorn app.main:app --reload --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000
```

or use the helper script:

```bash
./scripts/dev_backend.sh
```

runs on `http://localhost:8000`.

## docker alternative.

```bash
cd infra
docker compose up --build
```

this starts frontend, backend, and postgres together. the backend runs on port 8000, frontend on port 3000.

## stub mode.

set `USE_AI_STUBS=true` in your `.env` to run without any API keys. all AI calls return deterministic placeholder responses. good for UI development and testing.

## iris CLI (optional).

```bash
cd cli
pip install -e .
iris auth status
```

the CLI wraps the backend API for scripting and agent-driven editing. see [CLI reference](/reference/cli) for all commands.

## verification.

```bash
curl http://localhost:8000/api/health
curl http://localhost:8000/api/ai/health
npx tsc --noEmit --project frontend
```

all three should pass before you start developing.
